revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 長持山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市沢田 |位置= |形状= 円墳 |規模=径40ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=衝角付冑 馬具 挂甲 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''長持山古墳'''（ながもちやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[藤井寺市]]沢田に所在した[[円墳]]。家型[[石棺]]が墳長に露出していたので長持山の名ができたといわれる。2つの石棺が発掘されている。
== 概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが宮内庁の指定にもれ、ついに墳丘を失うに到っている。1877年（明治10年）頃、[[堺県]] [[県令]] [[税所篤]]が南側の石棺を発掘して、棺内に銅器、鉄剣があり、棺外の土中に甲が埋まっているのを見たという。1946年（昭和21年）大阪府と京都大学考古学研究室が北側にある別の石棺を調査し残存する遺物を検出した。この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家型石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3，4m、幅1ｍの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、衝角付冑、挂甲１具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも短甲などが遺存した。その他原位置を動かしているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から発見された。挂甲は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、漆甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家型石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺に、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお２つの石棺は現在、藤井寺市立[[道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。 ==参考文献 == *[[小林行雄]]編 「長持山古墳」『図解 考古学辞典』 東京創元社 １９７４年 第７版 750項～751項 ==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] ==外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html] 長持山古墳の石棺 藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課
* [[category:古墳]] * [[category:円墳]] * [[category:大阪府の古墳]] * [[category:藤井寺市]] * [[category:河内国]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 長持山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市沢田 |位置= |形状= 円墳 |規模=径40ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=衝角付冑 馬具 挂甲 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''長持山古墳'''（ながもちやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]沢田に所在した[[円墳]]。家型[[石棺]]が墳長に露出していたので長持山の名ができたといわれる。2つの石棺が発掘されている。
== 概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが、宮内庁の指定からもれ、墳丘を失うに至っている。

==概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが、宮内庁の指定からもれ、墳丘を失うに至っている。
1877年（明治10年）頃、[[堺県]][[県令]]の[[税所篤]]が南側の石棺を発掘して、棺内に銅器、鉄剣があり、棺外の土中に甲が埋まっているのを見たという。1946年（昭和21年）大阪府と京都大学考古学研究室が北側にある別の石棺を調査し残存する遺物を検出した。
==石棺と出土遺物== この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家型石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3.4メートル、幅1メートルの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、衝角付冑、挂甲1具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも短甲などが遺存した。その他原位置を動かしているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から発見された。挂甲は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、漆甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家型石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺にも、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお2つの石棺は現在、[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。
==参考文献 == *[[小林行雄]]編 「長持山古墳」『図解 考古学辞典』 東京創元社 1974年 第7版 750～751項
==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]]

==石棺と出土遺物== [[ファイル:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|250px|thumb|石棺]] [[ファイル:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|石棺の複製}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家形石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3.4メートル、幅1メートルの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、衝角付冑、挂甲1具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも短甲などが遺存した。その他原位置から移動しているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から検出された。挂甲は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、膝甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家形石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺にも、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお2つの石棺は現在、[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。
==参考文献 == *[[小林行雄]]編 「長持山古墳」『図解 考古学辞典』 東京創元社 1974年 第7版 750～751項
==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]]
==外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html 長持山古墳の石棺]（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課）

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 長持山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市沢田 |位置= |形状= 円墳 |規模=径40ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=衝角付冑 馬具 挂甲 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''長持山古墳'''（ながもちやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]沢田に所在した[[円墳]]。[[家形石棺]]が墳頂に露出していたので長持山の名ができたといわれる。2つの石棺が発掘されている。
==概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが、宮内庁の指定からもれ、墳丘を失うに至っている。
1877年（明治10年）頃、[[堺県]][[県令]]の[[税所篤]]が南側の石棺を発掘して、棺内に銅器、鉄剣があり、棺外の土中に甲が埋まっているのを見たという。1946年（昭和21年）大阪府と京都大学考古学研究室が北側にある別の石棺を調査し残存する遺物を検出した。
==石棺と出土遺物== [[ファイル:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|250px|thumb|石棺]] [[ファイル:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|石棺（複製）}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家形石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3.4メートル、幅1メートルの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、衝角付冑、挂甲1具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも短甲などが遺存した。その他原位置から移動しているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から検出された。挂甲は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、膝甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家形石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺にも、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお2つの石棺は現在、[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。
== 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 長持山古墳石棺 2基（考古資料）- 道明寺小学校所在。昭和49年3月29日指定<ref>[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html 指定文化財一覧（大阪府指定文化財・藤井寺市指定文化財）]（藤井寺市ホームページ）。</ref>。

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 長持山古墳 |画像=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |画像キャプション =復元された[[挂甲|小札甲]]と[[衝角付冑]]。
[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。 |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市沢田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=長持山古墳|mark=円墳.svg |marksize=28 |caption=遺跡位置 |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center}} |形状= 円墳 |規模=径40ｍ　 |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=衝角付冑、馬具、小札甲 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''長持山古墳'''（ながもちやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]沢田に所在した[[円墳]]。[[家形石棺]]が墳頂に露出していたので長持山の名ができたといわれる。2つの石棺が発掘されている。 ==概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが、宮内庁の指定からもれ、墳丘を失うに至っている。 1877年（明治10年）頃、[[堺県]][[県令]]の[[税所篤]]が南側の石棺を発掘して、棺内に銅器、鉄剣があり、棺外の土中に甲が埋まっているのを見たという。1946年（昭和21年）大阪府と京都大学考古学研究室が北側にある別の石棺を調査し残存する遺物を検出した。 ==石棺と出土遺物== この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家形石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3.4メートル、幅1メートルの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、[[衝角付冑]]、[[挂甲|小札甲]]（挂甲）1具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも[[短甲|板甲]]（短甲）などが遺存した。その他原位置から移動しているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から検出された。小札甲（挂甲）は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、膝甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家形石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺にも、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお2つの石棺は現在、[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。 ファイル:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|石棺 ファイル:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|石棺（複製）。[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 長持山古墳石棺 2基（考古資料）- 道明寺小学校所在。1974年（昭和49年）3月29日指定[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html 指定文化財一覧（大阪府指定文化財・藤井寺市指定文化財）]（藤井寺市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==参考文献 == *[[小林行雄]]編　「長持山古墳」『図解　考古学辞典』　　東京創元社　1974年　第7版　750-751頁 ==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] ==外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html 長持山古墳の石棺]（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課） {{デフォルトソート:なかもちやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群|消なかもちやまこふん]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=Nagamochiyama Kofun |画像=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |画像キャプション=Restored {{ill|lamellar armor|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, lamellar armor |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|lamellar armor|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The lamellar armor is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round-shaped kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]]

The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round-shaped kofun]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:長持山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|lamellar armor|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, lamellar armor |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|lamellar armor|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The lamellar armor is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round-shaped kofun]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[ja:長持山古墳]]

The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|lamellar armor|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The lamellar armor is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round-shaped kofun]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:長持山古墳]]

In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::01-17]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::挂甲]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:長持山古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:01-17]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:長持山古墳]]

Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "== Related Topics ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division)
