{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) revision.comment: Text replacement - "|衝角付冑}}" to "|衝角付冑|1=Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|lt=Horned helmet}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned helmet (shōkaku kabuto)|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue ware pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |形状=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751.== See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, Cultural Property Protection Division) [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:01-17]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |所在地=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |規模=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's designation, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze artifacts and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining artifacts. == Stone Coffins and Excavated Artifacts == The northern coffin is an ancient form of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. Artifacts unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern artifacts included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Cultural Properties == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Property ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Cultural Properties (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == *
