{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}}

Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}}

"Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

"Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built Period=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built Period=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built Period=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:長持山古墳 up to rev 98985534
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 長持山古墳 |画像=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |画像キャプション =復元された[[挂甲|小札甲]]と[[衝角付冑]]。
[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。 |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市沢田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|label=長持山古墳|mark=円墳.svg |marksize=28 |caption=遺跡位置 |position=bottom |width=200 |float=center}} |形状= 円墳 |規模=径40ｍ　 |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品=衝角付冑、馬具、小札甲 |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''長持山古墳'''（ながもちやまこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]沢田に所在した[[円墳]]。[[家形石棺]]が墳頂に露出していたので長持山の名ができたといわれる。2つの石棺が発掘されている。 ==概要== [[市ノ山古墳]]（伝[[允恭天皇]]陵古墳）の後円部の西南に位置し、その陪塚とみられるが、宮内庁の指定からもれ、墳丘を失うに至っている。 1877年（明治10年）頃、[[堺県]][[県令]]の[[税所篤]]が南側の石棺を発掘して、棺内に銅器、鉄剣があり、棺外の土中に甲が埋まっているのを見たという。1946年（昭和21年）大阪府と京都大学考古学研究室が北側にある別の石棺を調査し残存する遺物を検出した。 ==石棺と出土遺物== この石棺は蓋・身ともに両端に大きな円形突起を有する古い形式の家形石棺で、川原石を積んだ長さ3.4メートル、幅1メートルの竪穴式石室に納められていた。石室の北部棺外に、[[衝角付冑]]、[[挂甲|小札甲]]（挂甲）1具、[[鞍]]、[[鐙]]、轡、杏葉などの[[馬具]]類、刀、[[矛]]、鏃、鍬などが副葬され、南部棺外にも[[短甲|板甲]]（短甲）などが遺存した。その他原位置から移動しているが、帯金具破片、ガラス小玉、須恵器片などが棺の内外から検出された。小札甲（挂甲）は稀にみる完備したもので、肩甲、膝甲、籠手、臑当（すねあて）などを添え、鐙は木心鉄板張、鞍金具は[[金銅]]製である。南側の家形石棺は身に突起がなく、北側の石棺より新しい形式に属する。この石棺にも、もともと石室があったか否かは不明であるという。なお墳丘には[[円筒埴輪]]を廻らしていた。なお2つの石棺は現在、[[藤井寺市立道明寺小学校]]に保存されている。 ファイル:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|石棺 ファイル:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|石棺（複製）。[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。 == 文化財 == === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 長持山古墳石棺 2基（考古資料）- 道明寺小学校所在。1974年（昭和49年）3月29日指定[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html 指定文化財一覧（大阪府指定文化財・藤井寺市指定文化財）]（藤井寺市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==参考文献 == *[[小林行雄]]編　「長持山古墳」『図解　考古学辞典』　　東京創元社　1974年　第7版　750-751頁 ==関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] ==外部リンク == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html 長持山古墳の石棺]（藤井寺市教育委員会文化財保護課） {{デフォルトソート:なかもちやまこふん}} [[Category:古市古墳群|消なかもちやまこふん]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[category:円墳]]

Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|12=draft}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=draft}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=draft}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|12=draft}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=draft}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|12=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{citation needed|Nagamochiyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:挂甲]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}.
Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|qq=}}. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of {{ill|Ichinoyama Kofun|ja|市ノ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinoyama Kofun|qq=}} (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, {{ill|stirrups|ja|鐙|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stirrups|qq=}}, bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]
