Exhibited at the {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|ja|大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum|qq=}}. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}.
Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}.
Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[en:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]]

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]]

However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]]

The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}.
Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=Nagamochiyama Kofun |image=[[File:Asuka Museum Keikô.jpg|180px]] |imageキャプション=Restored {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} and {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}.
Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[Fujidera City]], Sawada |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]]

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nagamochiyama Kofun]] [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[sn:agamochiyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two stone coffins have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern stone coffin, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped stone coffin with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two stone coffins are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=|1=Dōmyōji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347336}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Stone coffin File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the stone coffin. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two stone coffins from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11653015}}

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two Sarcophaguss have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern Sarcophagus, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped Sarcophagus with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two Sarcophaguss are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=|1=Dōmyōji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347336}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the Sarcophagus. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two Sarcophaguss from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11653015}}

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two Sarcophaguss have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern Sarcophagus, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped Sarcophagus with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|qq=|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two Sarcophaguss are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|qq=|1=Dōmyōji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347336}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the Sarcophagus. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two Sarcophaguss from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] {{wikidata link|Q11653015}}

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two Sarcophaguss have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern Sarcophagus, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped Sarcophagus with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two Sarcophaguss are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|1=Dōmyōji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347336}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the Sarcophagus. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two Sarcophaguss from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] {{wikidata link|Q11653015}}

Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |位置={{coord|34|34|22.0|N|135|36|53.8|E|display=title,inline}} |地図2={{location map|Japan Osaka|coordinates=34|coordinates=34|coordinates=22.0|lon_deg=135|lon_min=36|lon_sec=53.8|map_dot_label=Nagamochiyama Kofun|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|caption=Site location|position=bottom|width=200|float=center}} |type=Round-shaped kofun |area=Diameter: 40m |built=5th century |Burial Facility= |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Horned helmet, horse gear, Lamellar armour |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Nagamochiyama Kofun''' (長持山古墳) is a round-shaped [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Sawada, [[Fujidera City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The name "Nagamochiyama" derives from the {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} exposed at the mound's summit. Two Sarcophaguss have been excavated from the site. == Overview == The kofun is situated southwest of the rear circular section of [[:en:Ichinoyama Kofun|Ichinoyama Kofun]] (allegedly the burial site of Emperor Ingyō) and is considered an associated satellite mound. However, it was excluded from the Imperial Household Agency's Historic Site, and its mound has been lost. In approximately 1877, Saisho Atsushi, the governor of [[:en:Sakai Prefecture|Sakai Prefecture]], excavated the southern Sarcophagus, finding bronze burial goods and an iron sword inside, as well as armor buried in the surrounding soil. In 1946, the Osaka Prefecture Board of Education and Kyoto University Archaeology Laboratory investigated another coffin on the northern side, recovering remaining burial goods. == Stone Coffins and burial goods == The northern coffin is an ancient type of house-shaped Sarcophagus with large circular protrusions on both ends of the lid and body. It was housed within a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} made of piled river stones, measuring 3.4 meters in length and 1 meter in width. burial goods unearthed outside the coffin include a {{ill|horned helmet|ja|衝角付冑|1=Horned Kabuto|lt=Horned helmet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horned helmet|13=User:Immanuelle/Horned Kabuto|qq=}}, a set of {{ill|Lamellar armour|ja|挂甲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lamellar armour|1=keikō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11077681}} with components like shoulder, knee, and shin guards, a saddle, [[:en:Stirrup|stirrups]], bits, leaf-shaped pendants, swords, spears, arrowheads, and farming tools like hoes. Southern burial goods included plate armor (short armor) and other items. Despite being looted, fragments of belt fittings, small glass beads, and pieces of Sue pottery pottery were found inside and outside the coffin. The Lamellar armour is notable for being exceptionally well-preserved, and the iron-core, wood-plated stirrups and gold-plated saddle fittings are particularly significant. The southern coffin, which lacks protrusions on the body, represents a more modern design. Whether it was also housed in a stone chamber remains unclear. Cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} surrounded the mound. The two Sarcophaguss are currently preserved at {{ill|Dōmyōji Elementary School|ja|藤井寺市立道明寺小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōmyōji Elementary School|1=Dōmyōji Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347336}} in Fujidera City. File:Nagamochiyama-kofun sekikan.jpg|Sarcophagus File:長持山古墳出土 刳抜式家形石棺.JPG|Replica of the Sarcophagus. Exhibited at the [[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum]]. == Historic Site == === Osaka Prefecture-Designated Historic Site === * Tangible designation1 ** Two Sarcophaguss from Nagamochiyama Kofun (archaeological materials) - Located at Dōmyōji Elementary School. Designated on March 29, 1974.[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/1387687920890.html Designated Historic Site (Osaka Prefecture and Fujidera City)] (Fujidera City website). == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Kobayashi, Yukio (ed.). "Nagamochiyama Kofun." *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 7th edition, 1974, pp. 750-751. == See Also == * [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]] == External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,1065,97,143.html Stone Coffins of Nagamochiyama Kofun] (Fujidera City Board of Education, designation1 Protection Division) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|長持山古墳|version=98985534|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:長持山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] {{wikidata link|Q11653015}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
